# Recommended Briefing Agenda: Max Bandwidth

## Thunderbolt Logistics | Full-Day Executive Briefing

---

**Visitor:** Max Bandwidth, SVP Digital Transformation, Thunderbolt Logistics
**VVIP Tier:** Gold
**Date:** TBD (pending scheduling)
**Location:** Raleigh Executive Briefing Center -- Data Center Room (live dashboard setup)
**Executive Sponsor:** Diana Kim, VP Transportation & Logistics
**Briefing Coordinator:** EBC Operations Team

---

### Agenda Overview

This briefing is engineered to produce a single tangible output: a board presentation kit Max can use in his Q3 2026 board meeting to win approval for the $2.5M AI expansion. Every session builds toward that goal. The day is data-first: live dashboards, real ROI numbers, validated pilot results, and customer reference stories with comparable data. No static slide decks. Everything Max sees must be exportable, shareable, and board-ready. The briefing culminates in a dedicated board presentation prep session where we hand over finished materials.

### Session Details

#### 9:00 AM -- Welcome & Chicago-Style Breakfast (30 min)

**Format:** Informal welcome in Data Center Room
**Protocol:** Gold VVIP -- EBC Manager greets at lobby
**Catering:** Chicago-style breakfast (because Chicago), double espresso, Lavazza coffee service, fresh fruit
**Setup:** Large primary display with live dashboard, whiteboards on wheels, multiple monitors for dashboard comparison

**Talking points for welcome:**
- Acknowledge his 100% SAB attendance and the validated $4M pilot ROI
- Light conversation: Chicago sports, his StarCraft gaming days, his dashboard obsession
- Set expectations: "Today is data-first. Everything you see is live, not a screenshot."

> **Note:** Max will look at dashboards on his phone during the welcome. This is normal. Do not take it as disengagement.

---

#### 9:30 AM -- AI Pilot Retrospective ($4M Savings Deep-Dive) (60 min)

**Format:** Data-driven review with live pilot dashboard
**Presenter:** AI Product Lead + Data Science SME
**Priority:** CRITICAL -- this session validates the ROI story

**Objective:** Walk through the Thunderbolt pilot results in full detail. Show the real data, the methodology, and how the $4M/year savings was calculated. This becomes the foundation of Max's board presentation.

**Content to cover:**
- Live dashboard showing pilot fleet performance (12% of fleet)
- Savings calculation methodology (validated by Thunderbolt finance team)
- Baseline vs. AI-optimized route metrics
- Unexpected findings and operational improvements
- Customer comparison data (from 2 other logistics customers)
- What would change at fleet-wide scale (scaling assumptions)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Pull latest data from Thunderbolt's pilot environment
- [ ] Prepare financial methodology document that Max can share with his board
- [ ] Have data science SME available for methodology questions
- [ ] Export-ready visualizations (for board presentation kit)

> **Critical:** Max will ask about the validation methodology. His board will scrutinize this. Be prepared to defend every assumption.

---

#### 10:30 AM -- Fleet-Wide Scaling Architecture (90 min)

**Format:** Architecture whiteboard + live dashboard
**Presenter:** Platform Engineering Team
**Tie to pipeline:** Directly supports the $2.5M Fleet-Wide AI Expansion opportunity

**Objective:** Present the fleet-wide scaling architecture that would support expanding the AI from 12% of the fleet to 100%. Include operational concerns to help Max address Helen Gearshift's resistance.

**Content to cover:**
- Architecture for scaling AI inference to 18,000+ vehicles
- Data pipeline from vehicles to central AI model
- Model training and retraining pipeline
- Rollback and safety mechanisms (important for Helen's concerns)
- Operational roles: where Operations still owns decision authority
- Gradual rollout plan with off-ramps at each stage

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Prepare architecture diagram with 18,000-vehicle topology
- [ ] Design rollback architecture explicitly (operational safety narrative)
- [ ] Prepare "AI augments Operations" narrative with specific examples

> **Strategic:** This session is designed to help Max counter Helen Gearshift's resistance. Frame the AI as augmenting Operations, not replacing it.

---

#### 12:00 PM -- Deep Dish Lunch Working Session (60 min)

**Format:** Working lunch with continued architecture discussion
**Catering:** Authentic Chicago deep dish pizza, Italian beef sandwiches, double espresso service
**Presenter:** Account Team + continuing architecture discussion

**Objective:** Keep momentum from the morning. Use lunch time to continue architectural and ROI discussions informally. Not a separate presentation.

**Content to cover:**
- Follow-up questions from morning sessions
- Thunderbolt's internal political situation and how to navigate it
- Board presentation strategy discussion
- Competitive intelligence Max needs about AWS SageMaker / Google Vertex

> **Note:** Max prefers informal lunch over formal plated meals. Buffet-style deep dish, casual seating, continued dashboard viewing on the primary display.

---

#### 1:00 PM -- Edge Computing for Real-Time Fleet Tracking (90 min)

**Format:** Technical deep-dive with live demo
**Presenter:** Edge Computing Engineering Lead
**Tie to pipeline:** Supports the $1.45M Edge Computing opportunity

**Objective:** Demonstrate how edge computing would enable real-time fleet tracking (current: 4 hour delay; target: sub-minute). This is the next phase after AI expansion.

**Content to cover:**
- Edge computing reference architecture for logistics
- On-vehicle telemetry processing
- Regional hub deployment for data aggregation
- Predictive maintenance use case
- Live dashboard showing what real-time fleet tracking would look like
- Cost/benefit analysis for the 18,000-vehicle fleet

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Build a live simulation dashboard for real-time fleet tracking
- [ ] Prepare architecture with on-vehicle + regional hub topology
- [ ] Cost analysis with ROI comparison to current batch telemetry

---

#### 2:30 PM -- Live Dashboard Demo Lab (60 min)

**Format:** Hands-on dashboard exploration
**Facilitator:** Senior Solutions Architect
**Setup:** Multiple live dashboards running simultaneously

**Objective:** Let Max explore the dashboards himself. He loves dashboards. Give him the controls.

**Lab exercises:**
1. Explore live pilot data dashboard (15 min)
2. Run "what if" scenarios in the ROI calculator (15 min)
3. Walk through the edge computing simulation dashboard (15 min)
4. Export board-ready visualizations (15 min)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] All dashboards running on live data
- [ ] ROI calculator pre-loaded with Thunderbolt baseline numbers
- [ ] Export functionality tested and ready
- [ ] Backup dashboards in case of demo issues

> **Note:** Max will want to take these dashboards with him. Prepare exportable links or his-team access before the session ends.

---

#### 3:30 PM -- Sponsor Drop-in: Diana Kim (30 min)

**Format:** Coffee meeting (Diana prefers casual format)
**Attendees:** Max Bandwidth, Diana Kim, Account Lead
**Protocol:** Gold VVIP -- sponsor drop-in required

**Talking points for Diana Kim:**
1. Acknowledge Max's pilot success and the validated ROI
2. Express appreciation for his SAB contributions
3. Discuss the board approval timeline and how Red Hat can support
4. Address Helen Gearshift's resistance strategically
5. Contract renewal conversation (November 2026)

> **Prep for Diana:** Brief her on the $4M pilot ROI numbers so she can congratulate Max credibly. She was at the Chicago SAB dinner in Feb -- reference that warm relationship.

---

#### 4:00 PM -- Board Presentation Prep Session (45 min)

**Format:** Working session to assemble board presentation kit
**Presenter:** Account Team + Analytics Team
**Priority:** HIGH -- this is the entire point of the briefing

**Objective:** Hand over a ready-to-use board presentation kit that Max can drop directly into his Q3 2026 board presentation. This is a deliverable, not a presentation.

**Kit contents to deliver:**
- ROI analysis slides (board-ready format, Thunderbolt branded if possible)
- Pilot data visualizations (exportable)
- Customer reference data (2-3 comparable customers)
- Competitive benchmark summary (AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI)
- Architecture diagrams (production-ready)
- Risk mitigation and rollback slides (for Helen's concerns)
- Financial methodology document (for board scrutiny)
- Executive summary template (Max can customize)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Pre-assemble the kit in advance of the briefing
- [ ] Review kit contents with Max during this session
- [ ] Customize any elements based on Max's feedback
- [ ] Deliver final kit in digital and printed formats

> **Critical:** This session converts the entire day's discussions into a tangible deliverable. Do not skip it.

---

#### 4:45 PM -- Wrap-up & Next Steps (30 min)

**Format:** Fast-paced close-out (Max likes fast wrap-ups)
**Presenter:** Account Lead

**Close-out checklist:**
- [ ] Confirm delivery of board presentation kit
- [ ] Summarize commitments and next steps
- [ ] Schedule follow-up 1 week after board meeting
- [ ] Gift presentation (Gold VVIP, logistics or sports-themed)
- [ ] Quick verbal NPS read

**Commitments to confirm:**
1. Board presentation kit delivery (same day)
2. Customer reference calls (schedule within 2 weeks)
3. Edge computing proposal timeline
4. Board meeting support (we stand ready to answer questions)
5. Post-board-meeting follow-up

| NPS Score | 9/10 or higher |
